produces.artifact: arc eval compare output (with token/time delta columns)

requirement.title: Capture Per-Trial Timing and Token Metrics
requirement.description: Every trial record in results.jsonl MUST include duration_ms (wall-clock), input_tokens, and output_tokens. The harness SHALL capture these at the moment the trial completes — this is the only point at which the subagent exit notification carries token accounting. Missing any of the three MUST be recorded as null (not omitted) so downstream aggregators can distinguish "zero" from "not captured".

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given any completed trial, its results.jsonl entry MUST contain the keys duration_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens. A value of null is allowed; a missing key is NOT. fr-gr-001

Given a trial that exits abnormally (timeout, crash), the harness MUST still write the partial record with duration_ms measured and the token fields set to null. The trial MUST still count toward k for verdict purposes. fr-gr-001

requirement.title: Surface Token and Duration Deltas in Benchmark Reports
requirement.description: The arc eval compare and arc eval report outputs MUST expose aggregated duration and token metrics alongside the pass-rate delta. For A/B runs, the delta SHOULD be presented as treatment mean minus baseline mean for each of duration_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens. This makes it visible when a skill improves quality but doubles cost — a regression that pass rate alone cannot catch.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a completed A/B run with timing and token data, arc eval compare MUST print duration_ms delta, input_tokens delta, and output_tokens delta in its summary section, in addition to assertion pass-rate delta. fr-gr-002

Given any delta in tokens or duration exceeding 100% (treatment doubles baseline), the compare output MUST visually mark it (flag, warning symbol, or equivalent) so the cost regression is not overlooked. fr-gr-002

requirement.title: discovered_claims[] Field in grading.json
requirement.description: The eval-grader agent MUST populate a discovered_claims[] array on grading.json for every trial. Each entry MUST have the shape { text, category, passed, evidence } where category is one of "factual" | "process" | "quality". Discovered claims are extracted from the output beyond the predefined assertions. They MUST NOT count toward the pass rate used by the verdict layer — they are advisory only, consumed downstream by arc eval audit.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a model-graded trial, grading.json MUST contain a discovered_claims[] array, possibly empty when the grader found no additional claims worth flagging. fr-gr-003

Given any discovered claim fails, the overall trial pass rate computed by the harness MUST be unchanged relative to the same grading.json with an empty discovered_claims[]. The pass rate depends only on the predefined expectations. fr-gr-003
Each discovered claim entry MUST include the required keys text, category, passed, evidence. Missing keys MUST fail grading.json schema lint. fr-gr-003

requirement.title: weak_assertions[] Field in grading.json
requirement.description: The eval-grader agent MUST populate a weak_assertions[] array on grading.json identifying predefined assertions the grader judges to be non-discriminative, ambiguous, or unverifiable from the available evidence. Each entry MUST reference the assertion id and include a "reason" explanation. These critiques MUST NOT affect the current trial's verdict — they are aggregated cross-trial by arc eval audit to suggest assertion improvements.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a grading pass, grading.json MUST include a weak_assertions[] array (possibly empty). Each entry MUST carry at minimum { assertion_id, reason }. fr-gr-004

Presence or absence of weak_assertions entries MUST NOT change the verdict verdict computation — the same trial graded twice with identical expectation scores but different weak_assertions MUST yield the same verdict. fr-gr-004

requirement.title: Blind-Comparator Auto-Trigger Rule
requirement.description: The harness MUST auto-invoke the eval-blind-comparator as a supplementary signal after the main A/B comparison if and only if ALL predefined assertions in the scenario are model-graded. Mixed grading (any code-graded assertion present) MUST suppress the blind run. Code-only scenarios MUST NOT invoke blind — the signal adds no value when assertions are deterministic.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a scenario whose assertions are all model-graded, arc eval ab MUST invoke the blind-comparator after the main grading completes and attach its preference-rate output to the report. fr-gr-005

Given a scenario that mixes code-graded and model-graded assertions, arc eval ab MUST NOT invoke the blind-comparator. The report MUST still show the main delta plus a note stating blind was skipped due to mixed grading. fr-gr-005
Given a scenario whose assertions are all code-graded, arc eval ab MUST NOT invoke the blind-comparator. The report MUST NOT mention blind at all. fr-gr-005
